parsed_review.summary:
CP00-195 (RP05 Matter Core, Risk C, 150 units, RP05.P08.M06.S18-RP05.P09.M07.S02) passes independent read-only review with no P0-P3 findings. Verified via Read/Grep/Glob only; no Bash, edits, writes, or connector calls.

SCOPE & DISTRIBUTION INTEGRITY: registry.js:2296 deliverable_counts {implementation:63,test:9,hermes_evidence:38,claude_review:21,security_audit:14,ui:5} and phase_counts {RP05.P08:73,RP05.P09:77} sum to 150 and match the prompt baseline and review-baseline.json exactly. micro_title_row_counts (registry.js:2283) match the prompt micro_title_distribution exactly. validateMatterCoreCp195Coverage (validators.js:5230) enforces pack_id/risk_class/unit_count/first+last unit/no-duplicate-IDs/RP05-only plus all four distributions (deliverable, phase, micro_phase, micro_title) and required micro-phases/unit titles. contracts/matter-core-contract.json current_pack is CP00-195 with matching evidence_review_handoff_terminal_bridge section.

NO-WRITE / NO-REAL-DATA BOUNDARY: A grep sweep for fetch/child_process/writeFile/appendFile/exec/spawn/process.env/network primitives across packages/matter/src returned zero matches, corroborating descriptor-only behavior at the code level. validateMatterCoreCp195EvidenceReviewHandoffTerminalBridge (validators.js:5299-5320) hard-rejects product-state writes, runtime permission evaluation, audit-event writes, review/approval route dispatch, rollback/retry execution, live DOM, API-handler execution, permission-decision-detail and audit-event-body leaks, and Loop engine. Descriptor enforces required terminal controls, customer-safe error codes, required outcomes (blocked permission_bypass_questions, review_required architecture_review_questions, approval_required human_approval_summary), security_audit/ui row counts, and leak guards. command-evidence.json: 8 commands all exit 0; no_real_data true; writes_product_state false.

AUTHORITY BOUNDARY HELD: validators.js:5315 rejects promotes_claude_to_final_approval; 5318 rejects claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation. construction-inspection.json sets claude_not_final_approver true and enterprise_trust_not_claimed_from_local_validation true. Claude review packet is read_only with allowed_tools Read,Grep,Glob. Matches required boundary: claude_role read_only_independent_reviewer, claude_is_final_approver false, human_final_approval_required_for_enterprise_trust_claim true.

HERMES = DETERMINISTIC CONTROL-PLANE ONLY: Hermes appears solely as deterministic evidence-packet/receipt labels (gate H05) and assert-based contract validation (scripts/validate-rp05-matter-core-contract.mjs); production_ready_candidate is computed locally as coverage.valid && bridge.valid and is explicitly distinct from enterprise trust. No Hermes runtime is embedded in product code.

NO OVERCLAIM: claude-review-result.json status pending_hardened_review; construction-inspection.json verdict PENDING with production_ready false; manifest/command-evidence report production_ready false. The pack does not assert enterprise production-readiness; it is correctly in pre-closeout state pending the full validation suite and human approval.

CP178+ REVIEW HARDENING: valid_review_receipts [] and invalid_review_attempts [] are both empty; the placeholder self-declares 'This placeholder is not valid review evidence' (raw_output stdout_bytes 0); readiness auth_preflight passed (exit 0, 1449-byte valid JSON result). No auth-failed, 0-byte, malformed, fenced-with-prose, or tool-call-shaped output is misrepresented as valid review evidence.

Final enterprise production-ready and enterprise-trust status remain correctly gated on the closeout-time full validation suite and human final approval, both outside Claude's authority. This review neither grants nor blocks that closure; it records no blocking findings.
